title: wp-bench base anchor: full 344-test run on the UNTRAINED foundation model (Qwen3-30B-A3B), vLLM bf16, same stack as the v1.2 gen-model rerun
purpose: The gen model's comparative story lacked a base anchor: v1.2 scored 0.4365 (fresh, BENCH-01) / 0.4484 (Gate-1) on wp-bench, but no untrained-base number existed on the same stack to show what the fine-tune actually bought. The judge already has its anchor (base: 0/121 parseable verdicts). This run gives the gen side the equivalent: the untrained foundation model measured on the identical harness, config, and seed.

served_model.role: untrained foundation base (NOT any merged/fine-tuned variant)
served_model.served_model_name: wp-30_70 (serve_30_70_vllm.sh hardcodes the served name regardless of MODEL_DIR; cosmetic)
served_model.task_token_note: The base lacks the extended <wp_gen>/<wp_judge> task-token embeddings; harness prompts carry <wp_gen> as plain text. That is the point of the anchor — the harness ran unmodified.

serving_stack.enable_thinking_mechanism: scripts/_wpbench_pth/usercustomize.py — same PYTHONPATH monkeypatch as every prior wp-bench measurement
serving_stack.driver: scripts/bench_wpbench_base_anchor.py (clone of the archived Phase 17 driver deprecated/scripts/bench17_wpbench_full_rerun.py with MODEL_DIR swapped to the base)

warmup_gate.result: PASS — non-empty completion observed before the wp-bench run started
warmup_gate.note: The warm-up helper (scripts/_p0_vllm_smoke_serve.generate) does not carry the enable_thinking=false patch, so the base's warm-up reply opened a <think> block — expected Qwen3 default behavior, gate only requires non-empty output. The wp-bench run itself applies enable_thinking=false via _wpbench_pth, identical to the v1.2 measurement.

wall_clock.includes: vLLM boot (~533s) + real-generation warm-up + full 344-test run + container teardown

comparison.same_stack_attestation: Confirmed same stack as the v1.2 fresh measurement (BENCH-01): vLLM engine, bf16 dtype, same image, same port 8020, same enable_thinking=false injection, same sampling config (temp 0.0, max_tokens 2048, timeout 1800s, concurrency 4, seed 1337), same wp-core-v1 344-test suite unlimited, same real-generation warm-up gate, same _run_wpbench() code path. Only MODEL_DIR differs. No GGUF/llama.cpp path anywhere.
comparison.honest_reading: The untrained base already scores 0.4033 on wp-bench, carried mostly by the knowledge MCQ split (0.469 of its performance vs 0.354 on execution) — Qwen3-30B ships with substantial WordPress knowledge out of the box. The gen fine-tune's lift is +3.32pp over base (fresh-vs-fresh, same stack) or +4.51pp vs the Gate-1 figure, both inside the project's own 5.20pp seed-noise floor. On this benchmark the fine-tune's measurable lift over base is modest; the training's demonstrated value is concentrated in the judge (base: 0/121 parseable verdicts, trained: full rubric compliance), not in a large wp-bench gen delta.
